rag_rules: Fix AGREE diagnostics by resolving undefined identifiers and correcting type mismatches in expressions.
Remove or rewrite AGREE clauses that reference unresolved AADL property names or invalid types.
Ensure all AGREE expressions use only visible identifiers from the current component scope.
Delete invalid guarantee statements that reference implementation-level features in type scopes.
generic_rules: Validate type consistency in AGREE expressions: numeric, boolean, and package-qualified references must match available model units.
Place component type contracts in type scopes and implementation bindings in implementation scopes.
Use only declared or visible identifiers in AGREE annexes; avoid introducing undefined references.
When AGREE reports type errors, align helper declarations with the expected expression types.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Guarantee expressions must be valid Boolean expressions with matching types.
Constants and equations in AGREE should use int, real, or bool as scalar types.
Assignments in AGREE must reference valid output features or declared variables.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new identifiers or types not present in the AADL model when fixing diagnostics.
Do not place implementation-level guarantees in component type annexes.
Do not encode AADL property associations (e.g., Stack_Size) as AGREE variables.
Do not assume that unresolved references can be resolved by adding new declarations.
irrelevant_topics: ARINC653 property associations and error handling actions
HM_Errors, HM_Process_Recovery_Actions, and related recovery actions
